violations.text: The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a cart. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pasta was observed hot in the refrigerator in deep lexan containers. Food was placed into shallow containers during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/20/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -gaskets for the hot holding unit -range radiant elements for char grill -2 door slider for table top refrigerator (out of service prior to inspection) Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

The following was observed in poor repair: -Gaskets for the hot hold unit -The metal vessel for the scale -Caulking behind the 3-bay sink -Multiple lightbulbs under hood systems -The partition seal on the deli area hand sink Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/24/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back, and the entrance to the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Wood is used in the construction of prep table in the back and on the panini line Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Two Staff members were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Employees dawned hair restraints during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the gaskets on lowboys and refrigeration units throughout the kitchen ware observed with an unidentifiable black and pink substance. Gaskets shall be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the hood system, Food Warmers and celling to Produce Walk-in were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Areas need to be cleaned on a more frequent basis. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
